Facts.text: Roman Emperor Galerius issues the Edict of Toleration, ending the persecution of Christians in the Roman Empire
Licinius unifies the entire Eastern Roman Empire under his rule
Islamic conquest of Iberia: Moorish troops led by Tariq ibn Ziyad land at Gibraltar to begin their invasion of the Iberian Peninsula (Al-Andalus)
French chamberlain Enguerrand de Marigny is hanged on the public gallows at Montfaucon after being convicted of sorcery
Black Death Massacre: Jewish community of Radolfzell, Germany, exterminated
Crusade of Nicopolis: one of the last large-scale medieval crusades departs from Dijon led by the Count of Nevers, eldest son of the Duke of Burgundy
Orbital calculations suggest that on this day, Pluto moved inside Neptune's orbit until July 23, 1503
Spain announces it will expel all Jews
Philip of Burgundy & England sign trade agreement
Jean Ribault and colonists arrive in Florida, the first French colonists in North America
The spire of Beauvais Cathedral, France, which made it the tallest human-made monument in the world at the time, collapses (never rebuilt)
First theater performance in America, the Spanish comedy "Rio Grande"
King James Iof England leaves Brielle
French rebel Henri de la Tour Turenne signs treaty with Spain
Tsjeng Tsj'eng-Kung begins siege of Dutch fort Zeelandia, Formosa
Naval battle in Bay of Barbados between attacking Dutch fleet led by Michiel de Ruyter damages ships but fails to take the English-held fort
Petar Zrinski, the Croatian Ban from the Zrinski family, is executed for treason
William Congreve's Restoration comedy "Love for Love" premieres in London
Game of Billiards is mentioned in New England Courant
Treaty of Aix-la-Chapelle drawn up ending the War of the Austrian Succession
Pope Clement XIV proclaims a universal jubilee for the second time
Dutch Colonial troops occupy guerilla leader Boni's maroon village in Suriname
The Battle of Boulou: French forces defeat a Spanish army under General Union during the War of the Pyrenees
US Department of the Navy forms
The New Hague Theater opens in The Hague, Netherlands
First practical typewriter is finished by Italian Pellegrino Turri
(Eastern) Louisiana admitted as 18th US state
Nicaragua declares independence from the Federal Republic of Central America
San Jose State University forms
Navaho indians attack Fort Defiance (Canby)
Swift Run Gap skirmishes in West Virginia
Mexican forces attack the French Foreign Legion at Hacienda Camarón, Mexico
Battle of Jenkin's Ferry, Arkansas; Confederate General William Reid Scurry is killed
New York becomes 1st state to charge a hunting license fee
Hawaiian YMCA organized
The Camp Grant Massacre of Apache Indians in Arizona Territory is committed by white and Mexican adventurers, 144 die
The Metropolitan Museum of Art opens its new premises designed by Calvert Vaux and Jacob Wrey Mould at its current site on Fifth Avenue and 82nd Street
Henry Lee Higginson starts the "Popular Music" series with the Boston Symphony, which evolves into the Boston Pops Orchestra in 1900
1st game played at Broad & Huntingdon St Park (Baker Bowl) in Philadelphia; Phillies beat Giants 19-10
Hailstones allegedly as big as oranges kill 246 people and some 1,600 sheep and cattle in Moradabad, India
First US national holiday on the centennial of Washington's inauguration
Championship wrestling match at Met turns into a brawl
Casey Jones dies heroically in a train wreck at Vaughn, Mississippi, while driving Cannonball Express, immortalized in "Ballad of Casey Jones"
The "Hawaiian Organic Act" is enacted by US Congress making Hawaii a US territory
NY Highlanders (Yankees) inaugural home opener at Hilltop Park, Manhattan; beat Washington Senators, 6-2
Ice cream cone makes its debut at St. Louis World's Fair invented by Ernest A. Hamwi (independently of other claimant Italo Marchiony in NY)
First official soccer game between neighboring countries Belgium and the Netherlands; the Dutch win 4-1 at Olympic Stadium, Antwerp
Honolulu, Hawaii, becomes an independent city
Cleveland Naps Addie Joss limits St.Louis Browns to 8 hits in 2-1 victory
Portugal approves female suffrage
Germany ratifies a bill introducing Daylight Saving Time, the first country in the world to do so
Orange Nassau soccer team forms in Groningen
Phillies beat Brooklyn Dodgers 9-0 in 20 innings
The British Government ends military conscription
1st American Professional Football Association Championship (1920): undefeated Akron Pros awarded inaugural title
American Professional Football Association reorganizes in Akron, Ohio
Chicago pitcher Charlie Robertson throws a perfect game as the White Sox beat Detroit Tigers, 2-0 at Navin Field
Automaker Dodge Brothers, Inc is sold to Dillon, Read & Company for $146 million plus $50 million for charity
Cherkess Autonomous Region forms in RSFSR (until 1957)
Ernst Streeruwitz becomes Chancellor of Austria, lasts less than 5 months
Thorvald Stauning becomes prime minister of Denmark for the second time
Austria gets "Austrian fascist" constitution
World Congress for Women's Rights concludes in Istanbul
The Philippines holds a plebiscite for Filipino women on whether they should be granted the right to suffrage; over 90% vote in the affirmative
Tropicana ballet of Havana, Cuba, forms

Air New Zealand then known as TEAL makes its inaugural flight with a flight from Auckland to Sydney. Later becomes 1st airline in the world to boil hot water in-flight to offer customers hot tea and coffee.
Brooklyn Dodger Tex Carleton no-hits Cincinnati Reds, 3-0
1st submarine built on Great Lakes launched, (Peto), Manitowoc, Wisconsin
Bergen-Belsen Concentration Camp for Jews forms
Dutch strike against forced labor in Nazi Germany's war industry
NY Giant Phil Weintraub gets 11 RBIs
Concentration camp Munchen-Allag freed
Record 48 U-boats sunk by the Allies this month
Red Army occupies Demmin
Soviet Army frees Ravensbruck concentration camp
US troops attack the Elbe
Boulder Dam renamed in honor of Herbert Hoover
Organization of American States charter signed at Bogotá, Colombia
US performs atmospheric nuclear test at Enewetak Atoll
Mr. Potato Head is the first toy advertised on television
Cuban bandleader Pérez Prado's mambo version of "Cherry Pink & Apple Blossom White" goes #1 for 10 weeks
Element atomic number 101, Mendelevium, announced
Imperial Bank of India nationalized
West German unions protest for a 40-hour workweek and higher wages
"Tossin' and Turnin'" single released by Bobby Lewis (Billboard Song of the Year, 1961)
First shuttle flights between Washington, D.C., Boston, and NYC begin (Eastern)
Anton LaVey founds the Church of Satan in San Francisco, California
MLB Baltimore Orioles' Stu Miller & Steve Barber lose 2-1 despite no-hitting Tigers
3 Oriole pitchers walk 14 NY Yankees in a 9 inning game
Cincinnati Reds pitcher Jim Maloney records his second MLB no-hitter in 10-0 rout of Houston Astros
WEDB TV channel 40 in Berlin, New Hampshire (PBS) begins broadcasting
MLB Chicago Cubs Billy Williams is 1st National League player to appear in 1,000 consecutive games
US troops invade Cambodia
"Arthur GodfreyTime" ends a 27 year run on radio
Women's tennis groups end disputes over sanctioning tournaments
North Vietnamese troops capture Saigon, ending the Vietnam War
"Silly Love Songs" single released by Wings (Billboard Song of the Year, 1976)
Royal Canadian Mint opens a branch in Winnipeg, Manitoba
The Betsy Ross Bridge, spanning the Delaware River from Philadelphia, Pennsylvania to Pennsauken, New Jersey opens
Ron Cey sets record of 29 RBIs in April
Juliana, Queen of the Netherlands, abdicates; Princess Beatrix Wilhelmina Armgard (42) becomes Queen Beatrix
Ronald Harwood's play "The Dresser" premieres in London with Freddie Jones and Tom Courtenay
Terrorists seize Iranian Embassy in London
Alvaro Magana chosen to succeed Jose N Duarte as President of El Salvador
Atlanta Braves win record 12th straight from beginning of season
Iranian offensive in Khusistan
1,700 skiers participate in an alpine event at Are, Sweden
Strong winds cause a 30 min delay in Toronto Blue Jays game
"Shoah", French documentary about the Holocaust, directed by Claude Lanzmann, starring Richard Glazar and Raul Hilberg, premieres in Paris
France performs nuclear test at Mururoa Atoll
Last edition of Brink Daily Mail and Sunday Express in South Africa
NFL Draft: Virginia Tech defensive end Bruce Smith first pick by Buffalo Bills
Ashrita Furman performs 8,341 somersaults over 12 miles
Mariners strike out 16 times, set record of 36 in 2 consecutive games
Lou Lamoriello is named NJ Devils President
NJ Devils beat Caps 3-2 taking 7th game of Patrick Division final
NY Knights 1st arena football game beats Cobras 60-52 (10,157 in LA)
The then largest banana split ever, at 4.5 miles long, is made along Market Street in Selinsgrove, Pennsylvania
World Exposition, Expo 88 opens in Brisbane Australia
Chicago newspaper movie critics Gene Siskel and Roger Ebert film their 500th movie review television show
US beats Costa Rica 1-0, in 3rd round of 1990 soccor World Soccer tournament
US hostage Frank Reed is freed after four years in the hands of pro-Iranian captors
The source code of the World Wide Web is released by CERN, making it freely available to all
Virgin Radio broadcasts for the first time in the United Kingdom.
After 120 years the last 15 A & S department stores close
Dutch Itallian Beppo-SAX launches from Cape Canaveral
Howard Stern Radio Show premieres in Grand Rapids, Michigan on WKLQ 94.5 FM
Atlanta Braves win record 19 games in April
Big Ben stops at 12:11 PM for 54 minutes
President Clinton's daughter Chelsea chooses to attend Stanford College
Tajik Prest Imomali Rakhmonov wounded in assassination attempt
Tino Martinez hits record 34 RBIs in April
Cambodia joins the Association of Southeast Asian Nations (ASEAN) bringing the total members to 10
PopeJohn Paul IIcanonizes Polish nun Sister Faustina [Helena Kowalska] as a Catholic saint
The Mitchell Report on the Arab-Israeli conflict is published
U.S. media release graphic photos of American soldiers abusing and sexually humiliating Iraqi prisoners at Abu Ghraib prison
Heidi Klumlaunches her clothing line for Jordache
Chrysler automobile company files for Chapter 11 bankruptcy
Failed attack on the Dutch Royal Family results in 7 deaths and 17 injured
The United Kingdom formally ends combat operations in Iraq
Manchester City defeats Manchester United 1-0 in what is claimed to be the biggest match in English Premier League history
Overloaded ferry in the Brahmaputra River, India, killing 103 people
Spain's economy double dips after a 0.3% contraction and 25% unemployment rate
The unfinished One World Trade Center overtakes the Empire State building to become the tallest building in New York
German KingHenry IVgives away Utrecht county of West Friesland
Christopher Columbusis given royal commission by Spanish monarchsIsabella IandFerdinand IIto equip his fleet to the New World
Henry VIIIof England andFrancis I of Francesign the Treaty of Westminster, pledging to combine their forces against Holy Roman EmperorCharles Vin attempt to win War of the League of Cognac
EmperorCharles VI& King Philip IV of Spain sign Treaty of Vienna
Member of Parliament and journalistJohn Wilkesconfined in the Tower of London, charged with seditious libel
Chancellor Robert Livingston andJames Monroesign Louisiana Purchase Treaty in Paris at a cost of 15 million dollars, doubles the size of the USA
Anton Rubinstein's opera "Dmitri Donskoi" premieres in St Petersburg, Russia
AmericanPaul Morphy, widely regarded as the world's best chess player, retires after returning from a 10-month chess tour of Europe
Charles Dickens' "A Tale of Two Cities" is first published in the literary periodical "All the Year Round" in weekly installments until November 26
US PresidentAbraham Lincolnorders Federal Troops to evacuate Indian Territory (US Civil War)
-5/1] Battle of Snyder's Bluff, Virginia (part of the Vicksburg Campaign of the American Civil War):General Shermanbeaten by Confederate forces
Battle of Chancellorsville, Virginia: In a major victory GeneralRobert E. Lee's troops defeat larger Army of the Potomac under Major General Joseph Hooker,Stonewall Jacksonis fatally wounded
English physicist and head of the Cavendish laboratoryJ. J. Thomsonannounces his discovery of the electron in a lecture to the Royal Institution
Weighing in at 170 lbs British former world heavyweight boxing championBob FitzsimmonsKOs 270 lb American Ed Dunkhost in round 2 of a lop-sided bout in Brooklyn, New York
Claude Debussy's only completed opera "Pelléas et Mélisande" premieres at the Salle Favart in Paris, performed by the Opéra-Comique and conducted by André Messager
PopeBenedict XVencyclical "On Dante"
Princess Julianagets seat in Dutch Council of State
FDRbecomes 1st US President to appear on TV when NBC-RCA television broadcasts the opening of 1939 New York World's Fair
Lou Gehrigsets a MLB record playing his 2,130th consecutive and final game for the New York Yankees.
Noël Coward's play "This Happy Breed" premieres in London; written in 1939, production was delayed by WWII
NY Giant,Mel Ottscores 6 runs in 1 game drawing 5 walks for 4th time
"Arthur GodfreyTime" begins a 27 year run on CBS radio
Adolf Hitlercommits suicide along with his new wife Eva Braun in the Fuhrerbunker in Berlin as the Red Army captures the city
Grand AdmiralKarl Dönitztakes over the head of state position in Germany following Adolf Hitler's suicide
Lord Haw-Haw (William Joyce) calls for crusade against the Bolsheviks
Cleveland Indians pitcherBob Feller's 2nd career MLB no-hitter; beats New York Yankees, 1-0
Little-Bigger League changes its name toBabe RuthLeague
Darius Milhaud's 4th Concerto for Piano and Orchestra (Op. 295) premieres in Haifa, Israel
Ted Williamsis 10th major league player to get 1,000 extra-base hits
MLB San Francisco Giants outfielderWillie Maysbecomes just the 9th player in MLB history to hit 4 homers in one game in 14-4 win against the Braves in Milwaukee
PremierFidel Castroof Cuba receives Lenin-Peace Prize
NASA civilian pilotJoseph A. Walkertakes X-15 to 75,190 m
Paul McCartneyreleases "Red Rose Speedway" album, includes the hit single "My Love"
US President Nixon announces the resignation ofH. R. Haldeman, Ehrlichman and others
US PresidentRichard Nixonhands over partial transcripts of Watergate tape recordings
Muhammad Alibeats Jimmy Young in 15 for heavyweight boxing title
Human rights group Mothers of the Plaza de Mayo begin protesting at the forced disappearances of thousands, under the military dictatorship ofJorge Rafael Videla, in Buenos Aires
British film "The Stud" starringJoan Collins, based on the book by her sisterJackie Collinspremieres in UK
Dale Murphydrives in record tying 28th & 29th runs of April
Bill Elliottsets all-time NASCAR qualifying record, winning pole for the Winston 500 at Talladega Superspeedway; 212.809 mph (342.483 km/h; 44.998 seconds)
NY YankDave Winfieldgets his 29th RBI of April-Sets AL & ties ML Rec
PopeJohn Paul IIbeatifies Victoire Rasoamanarivo of Madagascar
As Met pitcherDavid Coneargues a call at 1st base, 2 Braves score
208th and final episode of "The Cosby Show" airs on NBC-TV
USPresident Clintonapproves the sale of $227 million of crude oil from the Strategic Petroleum Reserve; US gas prices are at their highest levels in 5 years
42 million watchEllen DeGenerespublicly declare she is gay
Rock for the Rainforest benefit concert held at Carnegie Hall, NYC; performers include:Sting,Elton John,James Taylor,Stevie Wonder,Lyle Lovett, Bobby McFerrin, Bonnie Raitt, Shawn Colvin and Zucchero
USVice President Cheneycalls for increased domestic production of fossil fuels and increased usage of nuclear power to meet America's energy demand
A referendum in Pakistan overwhelmingly approves the Presidency ofPervez Musharraffor another five years
"Mean Girls", teen comedy film released starringLindsay LohanandRachel McAdamsand written byTina Fey
German ChancellorAngela Merkelofficially hands over the Waldseemüller Map (oldest known document to name America) to the US Library of Congress
David Blainesets a new Guinness World Record for holding his breath underwater for 17 minutes 4-1/2 seconds on "TheOprah WinfreyShow"
Two skeletal remains found near Ekaterinburg, Russia, are confirmed by Russian scientists to be those ofAlexei Nikolaevich, Tsarevich of Russia, and one of his sisters
George Washingtonis inaugurated as the first President of the United States of America at Federal Hall in NYC
Red Army opens attack on German Reichstag building in Berlin
Ostankino Tower, the then highest free-standing structure in the world at 540m is finished in Moscow, Russia
K'ak Chan Yopaat [Smoke Snake], 11th ruler of the Maya city of Copán (578-628), born in Copán (d. 628)

Eugen Bleuler, Swiss psychiatrist (introduced the term schizophrenia to describe the disorder previously known as dementia praecox), born in Zollikon, Switzerland (d. 1940)

Max Nettlau, German anarchist and historian, born in Neuwaldegg, Austria (d. 1944)
Hans Poelzig, German architect (Grosses Schauspielhaus), born in Berlin (d. 1936)
Cyriel Verschaeve, Belgian priest and writer (Maria Magdalena - 1928), born in Ardooie, West Flanders, Belgium (d. 1949)
Orso Mario Corbino, Italian physicist and politician (Minister of Education), born in Augusta, Italy (d. 1937)
